description:
You are acting as a technical-writer who is building a tiny example for the “Scheduling with cron” section of an internal documentation set.  
Create one new Markdown file that shows a minimal, real-world cron entry.  

Final state that must exist after you finish:

1. Directory  
   /home/user/tech_writing/schedules/  
   (Create all parent directories if they do not already exist.)

2. File  
   /home/user/tech_writing/schedules/backup_cron.md  

   Exact required contents (two lines, Unix line-endings, **no trailing spaces**):

   ```
   # Nightly Documentation Backup
   0 2 * * * /usr/bin/rsync -a --delete /home/user/docs/ /home/user/docs_backup/
   ```

3. A simple log file confirming that you created the Markdown file:  
   /home/user/tech_writing/schedules/task_log.txt  

   The log must contain exactly one line (followed by a newline) with this text:

   ```
   backup_cron.md created
   ```

Verification details (what the automated checker will look for):
• The directory “/home/user/tech_writing/schedules” exists.  
• File “backup_cron.md” exists and its **entire** contents match the two-line block shown above, byte-for-byte.  
• File “task_log.txt” exists in the same directory and contains exactly the single line shown above and nothing else.

Use whatever single terminal command(s) you prefer—as long as the final on-disk state matches everything described here.  
Do not modify or delete anything outside /home/user/tech_writing/schedules.  
Do all work as the current non-root user.
truth:

3. /home/user/tech_writing/schedules/task_log.txt
